program.name: QBE Graduate Program
program.close_date_raw: Applications are now closed for the 2026 graduate program.
program.rotation_text: QBE says you start in your first rotation team from week two of the program (after induction week).

eligibility.work_rights: You need proof of unrestricted Australian working rights.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed a bachelor’s degree within two years before the program starts. QBE also accepts postgraduate qualifications completed within two years of the program start.
minimum_requirements: Submit a resume and a current academic transcript.
Provide proof of unrestricted Australian working rights.
A cover letter is not required.
Have completed a bachelor’s degree (or postgraduate qualification) within two years of the program start date.

stages.name: Application
stages.details: QBE’s recruitment timeline lists the application period as July.

stages.name: Assessment
stages.details: QBE’s recruitment timeline lists assessment as August.

stages.name: Selection
stages.details: QBE’s recruitment timeline lists selection as September.

stages.name: Online application
stages.details: You submit your resume and current academic transcript. QBE says a cover letter is not required.

stages.name: Online experience and assessments
stages.details: QBE lists ability, working style and personality assessments, plus learning about QBE and its values.

stages.name: Phone interview
stages.details: You speak with a member of the graduate recruitment team.

stages.name: Final assessment centre
stages.details: You complete a group activity and an individual interview. For technical streams, QBE says there may be an additional technical interview or case study.

stages.name: Probity checks and offer
stages.details: QBE lists probity checks, then an offer to join the graduate program.

tips: QBE says a cover letter is not required.
QBE suggests you research the program streams and email questions to the graduate team.
QBE says you will need proof of unrestricted Australian working rights when you apply.

exec_themes: Execute a clear strategy built around six priorities (including portfolio optimisation, sustainable growth, customer focus, and pace/efficiency).
Actively rebalance and simplify the insurance portfolio (including exiting or running off non-core lines) to reduce volatility and improve consistency.
Maintain strong underwriting discipline (risk selection, pricing/rate adequacy, and portfolio management) even as the market cycle changes.
Grow core businesses through targeted organic growth, while continuing portfolio optimisation.
Invest in modernisation and technology (including AI use cases) to improve customer and partner experience and operational agility.
Use disciplined capital management to return capital to shareholders (e.g., buybacks/dividends) while staying within regulatory capital targets.
commercial_context.profit_engine: QBE mainly makes money by (1) underwriting profit—pricing risk well so premiums collected exceed claims and operating costs, measured through a low combined operating ratio—and (2) investing the insurance float to earn investment income. QBE emphasises disciplined risk selection, monitoring premium rate adequacy, and active portfolio management to sustain profitable underwriting, alongside robust investment returns from a largely high-quality fixed income portfolio with a smaller allocation to risk assets.
commercial_context.headwinds: External and operating pressures discussed by QBE include: increased competition in some insurance lines (including property), premium rate increases moderating versus prior years (which can slow revenue growth), ongoing uncertainty from a dynamic economic backdrop and claims inflation risk, catastrophe/event risk (even if outcomes can be below or above allowance in a given year), and broader geopolitical/global trade uncertainty that can add volatility to markets and the operating environment.
commercial_context.esg: QBE frames ESG mainly through its sustainability strategy and reporting suite. It describes three sustainability focus areas: (1) taking action to support climate resilience and the transition to a net-zero economy, (2) enabling a sustainable and resilient workforce, and (3) partnering for growth through innovative, sustainable and impactful solutions (including work with the QBE Foundation and impact investing such as Premiums4Good). QBE also reports on human rights and modern slavery due diligence in its Modern Slavery and Human Trafficking Statement, including supplier risk assessments and governance oversight.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: In 2025, QBE describes a refreshed climate strategy: it reassessed its prior commitment to achieve net-zero emissions in its underwriting and investment portfolios by 2050, and replaced it with a renewed approach focused on actions within QBE’s control to support the transition to a net-zero economy. Separately, QBE highlights that portfolio optimisation progressed with non-core portfolio actions broadly concluding in 2025, leaving a more focused business with substantially less property catastrophe exposure, and that it is adjusting sustainability reporting by introducing an Impact Report supported by a data book and moving mandatory climate disclosures into the Annual Report’s Sustainability Report.

notes: Program name and streams were taken from QBE’s Graduate Program overview page.
The site states that applications are closed for the 2026 graduate program, but it does not provide an application open date or a specific close date.
No salary, program length, or full rotation schedule details were explicitly stated on the pages reviewed.

notes: Recruitment timeline months and the step-by-step application stages were taken from the Application process page.
The stages listed are described by QBE as the most common process and may vary by stream.

sources.title: Application process | QBE Graduate Program | QBE AU

notes: Most items are drawn from QBE’s FY25 Investor Report (strategy priorities, underwriting discipline, portfolio optimisation, competition, inflation/cat risk, investment portfolio positioning).
Sustainability and recent climate-strategy changes are drawn from QBE’s 2025 Impact Report and the reporting-suite descriptions within QBE documents.

Some themes (e.g., customer focus, pace/efficiency, employer-of-choice) are listed as priorities in the Investor Report; the response avoids adding details not explicitly described in the cited pages.

Where QBE describes market conditions (competition, inflation dynamics, geopolitical uncertainty), these are summarised as headwinds in plain English without quantifying impacts beyond what QBE states.

The exact wording and full detail of the six priorities and 2030 Sustainability Scorecard targets are broader than the excerpts reviewed; this summary captures the high-level items visible in the sourced sections.
